tldr: Burlington's Committee of the Whole held a two-day meeting covering major items including approving long-term land lease to Indwell for affordable housing, launching Vision Zero road safety planning, and refusing a proposed 11-storey residential development on Brant Street. The committee also addressed council remuneration, economic development service delivery, and heritage property designations.

items.title: St. Luke's Window to the Lake implementation and agreements
items.notes: Approved 20-year agreements with St. Luke's Church for the Window to the Lake initiative with up to $30,000 annual maintenance budget.

items.title: 2026 Tax Levy By-law
items.notes: Approved the 2026 tax levy by-law for setting municipal tax rates.

items.title: Financial status report December 31, 2025
items.notes: Received year-end financial status report for information purposes.

items.title: Significant Tree Maintenance Rebate Program
items.notes: Approved new rebate program with $50,000 funding from Tree Enhancement Reserve Fund.

items.title: Spruce Avenue Area Renewal tender award
items.notes: Awarded $12.2 million contract to King Paving & Construction for road renewal with 10-week road closure authorization.

items.title: Community Engagement Charter relaunch
items.notes: Endorsed and adopted updated Community Engagement Charter and Framework.

items.title: Waterdown Road properties lease to Indwell
items.notes: Authorized long-term land lease of 2,670 square metres to Indwell for affordable housing at nominal value, with Councillor Galbraith declaring conflict.

items.title: Council remuneration for 2026-2030 term
items.notes: Approved council compensation structure and directed report on intern programs from 2018-2026.

items.title: Economic Development and Tourism service delivery options
items.notes: Directed execution of service agreement with Burlington Economic Development and Tourism and planning for Community Economic Development Strategy.

items.title: Standing Committee membership eligibility criteria review
items.notes: Directed review of eligibility restrictions for registered lobbyists serving on committees with report back by Q3 2026.

items.title: 2026 calendar of meetings amendment
items.notes: Approved adding full week between committee and council meetings in 2026 schedule.

items.title: On-Demand Transit Pilot Project exploration
items.notes: Directed development of 24-month pilot project proposal for on-demand transit service with comprehensive analysis due Q4 2026.

items.title: 2025 Community Survey presentation request
items.notes: Directed presentation of 2025 Community Survey results from Deloitte to Q2 2026 committee meeting.

items.title: Operating budget performance report year-end 2025
items.notes: Received year-end financial position report and directed allocation of retained savings.

items.title: Government Relations annual report and funding updates
items.notes: Received information report on intergovernmental advocacy and funding work.

items.title: Advisory Committee annual reports and 2026 workplans
items.notes: Approved 2026 advisory committee workplans and budgets, received 2025 annual reports.

items.title: Confidential legal update on Burlington New Official Plan litigation
items.notes: Provided confidential instructions to legal counsel regarding Official Plan litigation matter.

items.title: Vision Zero Road Safety Action Plan
items.notes: Approved $300,000 from Capital Purposes Reserve Fund to hire consultant for Vision Zero plan development due Q4 2027.

items.title: Heritage designation candidates Phase II shortlist
items.notes: Directed heritage assessment of 10 properties for potential designation under Ontario Heritage Act with reports due before January 1, 2027.

items.title: Provincial Land and Development Facilitator request for 1200 King Road
items.notes: Formally requested Provincial facilitator appointment to coordinate land-use negotiations for 1200 King Road property.

items.title: Official Plan and Zoning amendments for 1056-1060 Brant Street
items.notes: Refused applications for 11-storey, 93-unit residential building after statutory public meeting and delegations.

items.title: Draft Housing Community Improvement Plan Amendment
items.notes: Directed consideration of feedback from statutory public meeting before bringing forward affordable rental housing CIP amendments.

items.title: Information items package
items.notes: Received and filed seven information items including forecasts, presentations, and delegation materials.
